text:
MARKET TODAY (June 23): Rough open. A global chip selloff is hammering tech — South Korea's market fell almost 10% overnight, and US futures followed. Nasdaq is down about 2%, the S&P about 1.2%, the Dow near flat. The trigger: signals that the chip rally got overheated, plus fresh doubts about AI spending. Micron is down ~10% ahead of its earnings this week. Oil is also down ~1.5%.

YOUR HOLDINGS: You're ~90% chips/tech and using margin, so a day like this stings extra. The ones to think about:
- PLTR (you're down ~$1,131, -8.7%): the tool flipped it to SELL — its uptrend has broken below its key average line. Worth considering trimming or exiting. If it bounces back toward ~138, that's a nicer exit price.
- AVGO (down ~$5,003, -12.7%): TRIM signal — it slipped under its average line and is losing steam, and it's a chip name right in today's crosshairs. Consider lightening up; sell the rest if it falls under 336.
- SMH (chip ETF, still a winner +12.5% / +$1,734): structure is fine, but it IS the center of today's storm. Watch your 540 floor.
- SPCX (brand-new, very volatile IPO, basically flat today): it just logged one of its biggest one-day drops on record and is wobbling again. Too new for the tools — manage it by hand; your floor is 150.
- Everything else (SPY, QQQ, QQQI, SNOW +49% winner, CRWD, PTNM) looks fine today.

BUY IDEAS: Honestly, nothing I'd chase. The scanner flagged ASML, SOXL and ANET — but ASML and SOXL are chips (SOXL is leveraged chips) on the exact day the sector is bleeding, which is catching a falling knife, especially since you're already overweight chips and on margin. ANET (networking, not chips) is the only one I'd even watch — buy near 161, bail under 160.5 — and even that I'd let settle first.

BOTTOM LINE: This is a defense day — your one real decision is whether to lighten PLTR and AVGO; skip new chip buys while the sector is selling off.
